dependencies: Concentration on pork, turkey, beef, chicken, and nuts as principal raw materials
Majority of turkeys raised by the Company
Purchases hogs for Austin, Minnesota processing facility
Dependent on suppliers to meet raw material needs as Company shifts toward value-added portfolio
risks: Fluctuations in grain prices and fuel costs affecting turkey production
Certain raw materials such as cashews sourced internationally causing additional risks to pricing and availability
Increased dependence on suppliers as Company shifts focus toward value-added portfolio
Outbreaks of disease among livestock and poultry flocks (ASF, BSE, PCV2, PRRS, FMD, PEDv, HPAI)
HPAI detected within turkey supply chain in fiscal 2024 and first quarter of fiscal 2025
Fluctuations in commodity prices and availability of raw materials
Decreased availability or less favorable pricing for nuts, tomatoes, avocados, or other produce due to poor growing conditions
Supplies of natural and organic proteins may impact ability to ensure continuing supply
International trade barriers and restrictions could result in decreased foreign demand and increased domestic supply

regulatory_risks: Regulation by U.S. Department of Agriculture
Regulation by U.S. Food and Drug Administration
Regulation by U.S. Department of Homeland Security
International, federal, and state taxing authorities regulations
Workforce mobility regulations
Animal welfare regulations
Food safety regulations
Processing, packaging, storage, distribution, advertising, and labeling regulations
Maximum production line speeds for pork harvest facilities under review by government
Loss of availability of government inspectors due to government furlough
New or modified laws, regulations, and accounting standards
Stringent environmental laws and regulations pertaining to discharge of materials and handling of wastes
Compliance with foreign regulations and tax laws
Compliance with U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act
Enforcement of remedies in foreign jurisdictions
Class actions involving employees, consumers, competitors, suppliers, shareholders
Claims relating to product liability, contract disputes, antitrust regulations, intellectual property, advertising, labeling, wage and hour laws, employment practices, environmental matters
Pork Antitrust Litigation
Turkey Antitrust Litigation
Poultry Wages Antitrust Litigation
Red Meat Wages Antitrust Litigation
Tax deficiency notices from State of São Paulo Tax Authority Office in Brazil for ICMS and ICMS-ST taxes
Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 - 15% corporate minimum tax
OECD Pillar Two Framework - minimum effective tax rate of 15%

sanction_risks: Military conflicts in Russia, Ukraine, and Middle East causing inflated fuel costs and supply chain shortages

Potential for additional supply chain disruptions, rising prices for oil and commodities, volatility in capital markets and foreign exchange rates, rising interest rates, heightened cybersecurity risks due to geopolitical conflicts
International trade barriers and restrictions could result in decreased foreign demand
Imposition of tariffs, quotas, trade barriers and other restrictions on international sales
Potential political tariffs as risk to International segment outlook
